title: Use unnamed variable pattern for unused exception parameters
description: When an exception parameter is not used in a catch block, the unnamed variable pattern should be used. This improves code clarity and signals intent to readers and tools, helping maintain clean and modern Java code.
recommendation: Replace unused exception parameter names with the unnamed variable pattern '_' in catch blocks.

noncompliant: catch (Exception e) { /* e is not used */ }

checks.message: Use unnamed variable pattern for unused exception parameters instead of naming them.
